events.label: assistant
events.text: Wrote the assessment to `/app/answer.json`. Summary: aluminum conductor landed on a device not marked CO/ALR is a code violation of NEC 110.14(A) (termination listing requirement, distinct from the sizing/ampacity rules in NEC 310 / Table 310.16). This is a `fail` at `high` risk — dissimilar-metal termination on aluminum leads to oxidation, creep, and loosening, a known fire/shock hazard — and must be corrected (device swap to a CO/ALR-rated device or a listed AL/CU pigtail connector) before rough-in is signed off.
